column5: A form of ovarian dysgenesis@ a disorder characterized by lack of spontaneous pubertal development@ primary amenorrhea@ uterine hypoplasia@ and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism as a result of streak gonads. ODG4 is an autosomal recessive condition.
column33: XX ovarian dysgenesis-short stature syndrome is a rare genetic disorder primarily affecting females. It is characterized by short stature and ovarian dysgenesis@ leading to absent or delayed puberty and infertility.
column34: Symptoms include short stature@ delayed or absent puberty@ primary amenorrhea@ uterine hypoplasia@ and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism. Patients may also experience fatigue and muscle weakness.
column35: The condition is caused by homozygous mutation in the MCM9 gene on chromosome 6q22. This mutation impairs the function of the MCM9 protein@ which is crucial for DNA replication and repair during cell division.
